original_image_tags: A typewritten letter on light-colored paper, with the word "COPY" typed vertically in the upper left corner. The letter is addressed to Mr. G. J. Rathman of the U.S. Atomic Energy Commission from the NEPA Project in Oak Ridge, Tennessee, dated 10 February 1949. The text concerns photographs of an unidentified flying object taken in July 1947 and an evaluation by the Air Force. The letter appears to be a carbon copy. The paper shows age-related wear and a slight crease or tear at the bottom left corner.

elements.description: A single page of a typed letter or memo on official stationary. The document contains typed text detailing a response regarding photographs of an unidentified object, along with a technical evaluation of the negatives. Visible text includes 'NEPA Project', '10 February 1949', 'Mr. G. J. Rathman', 'U. S. Atomic Energy Commission', 'Wright-Patterson Air Force Base', and a vertical 'COPY' stamp on the left and right margins. There are no visual elements such as photographs, sketches, diagrams, or charts embedded on the page.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree the page contains no embedded visual elements like photographs or diagrams, only typed text and stamps, and they align on the main quoted text elements like 'COPY' and key names/dates.
